title: Add timeout policy and an Actix handler
scenario: A handler adapter should translate request DTOs, call the use case, and return response data without pushing HTTP concepts into domain code.
instructions: Add register_user_with_timeout using tokio::time::timeout and a TimedOut error. Then add a thin Actix handler using web::Data, web::Json, HttpResponse, and Responder. Keep HTTP status mapping in adapters and the use case free of Actix types.

hints.body: Timeout is application policy around the use-case future.
The handler should only extract inputs, call adapter/application code, and build an HTTP response.
Do not pass web::Json or HttpResponse into domain or application modules.

completionExplanation: The arc ends with cancellation-aware async application code and a thin Actix boundary that preserves module ownership.
